message:
feat(cli): add archive-aware SQLite reconciliation for read paths and canonical snapshot sources

Integrate archive drift detection and reconciliation into all CLI database open
paths so that stale SQLite indexes are automatically repaired before commands
read from them. Previously, only the pool init gate reconciled; now individual
CLI commands that open read-only or read-write connections also detect and fix
archive-ahead-of-DB drift.

Key changes:

- recover_sqlite_file_with_storage_root calls reconcile_sqlite_file_with_archive
  after backup restoration and healthy-file checks to catch project-level drift
  that backup restoration alone would not fix
- maybe_reconcile_sync_opened_sqlite_archive_drift inserted into all
  open_db_sync_with_database_url_and_storage_root_internal return paths to
  detect drift after initial open and re-open after reconciliation
- New CanonicalSnapshotSource abstraction encapsulates three source kinds
  (LiveSqlite, LiveSnapshot, ArchiveSnapshot) for read-only operations that
  need a consistent point-in-time view without holding a write lock
- resolve_canonical_snapshot_source_path chooses the best snapshot source based
  on archive drift, DB health, and file existence
- list-projects command uses open_db_sync_canonical_read_with_database_url for
  archive-aware reads
- robot.rs updated for structural changes in the CLI module

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
